tax: Placed at order rank, Magmatic Elementals is defined by composition and formation mechanics, not by colour, size, or danger: a fire-heated rock body, molten or glassy, not an open-combustion one. A new family belongs here only if it shows a distinct rock-heat state with its own formation and field signs.
ap: The grouping is recognised by a glowing lava lobe or an angular black-glass figure. A field surveyor confirms membership from at least one persistent marker — a cooled basalt lobe, scorched track, or a litter of glass flakes — which survives even when the body disperses.
eco: Magmatic Elementals occupies lava benches, thermal vents, and quenched obsidian margins. Persistence is elemental rather than reproductive: members form where vent heat melts basalt or a fast quench freezes it to glass and recur with the vent reheats or re-quenches the same rock, sustaining themselves on thermal-vent heat and residual rock-heat.
beh: Across the grouping, behaviour is the disturbance-response of the medium: members flow or stalk while warm and fall dormant as stone or glass when cooled, fooling crews into crossing. Roles, moods, and summoning circumstances are recorded on the entries below, not treated as new taxa.
traitsText: Shared characters: a rock-heat body, fire immunity, and a cooling-failure mode. These are grouping-level traits only; specific resistances, abilities, and field signs belong to each family and species beneath this node.
abilities: Grouping-level abilities are the broad capacities implied by the shared body: engulfing heat or cutting glass, rock-melting repair, and dormancy disguise. Named, bounded powers are assigned to the species that actually possess them.
bg: In the Throat, Magmatic Elementals matters because their dormant forms are walking benches and slag-figures that wake under crews' feet. It lets survey reports sort rock-heat hazards by state, so a crew knows whether to fear heat-flow or glass-shrapnel without collapsing distinct hazards into one vague label.
g: Magmatic Elementals is the order-rank grouping of fire elementals whose bodies are molten or cooled volcanic rock within the True Elementals. Its members share a body of fire-heated rock that carries its own heat and can persist by cooling rather than dying outright, and divide below into family groups by whether the rock is molten and flowing or cooled to mobile glass. Magmatic elementals bridge fire and earth, which is why their dormant forms fool crews into walking onto live benches.
